title: fixture + oracle test pattern is correct and should be defended
description: Every new method has a hand-written fixture (.json) with sessionState and independent body_json matchers. The oracle is the spec text, not the code output. Tests verify both request shape (body_json matcher) and response deserialization. This is the right pattern: a round-trip serde test would prove nothing. If a contributor suggests simplifying to just deserialize-and-assert, reject it — the body_json matcher catches request serialization bugs that round-trip tests miss.
close_reason: implemented; ofy.6 is a decision record no action needed
